form_answers.question: Is Health-club/Organizational Membership currently approved in the budget?
Does the membership provide opportunity for community inclusion?
Does the membership accommodate the individual’s health and/or safety needs?
Does the membership increase independence or substitute for human assistance?
Is the membership provided exclusively toward the benefit of the participant? (not family)
Is the organization open to the public and not a private/invitation-only club?

findings.criterion_text: Organization is open to the public — not a private/invitation-only club
findings.note: The page shows public access ('sign up').
A published price was found on the page: $10.

findings.criterion_text: Fee-match: published membership fee is consistent with the fee on the form (yearly/monthly)
findings.note: The website price ($10) differs from the fee on the form ($80).

findings.criterion_text: Memberships are approved in the participant's budget
findings.note: Depends on internal records (budget / Life Plan / documents) — not checkable from the public web.

findings.criterion_text: Membership relates to a goal in the participant's Life Plan
findings.note: Depends on internal records (budget / Life Plan / documents) — not checkable from the public web.

summary: Automated rule-based check of Brooklyn Museum's website found 2 of 3 website-verifiable items, with 0 needing manual review. No AI model was used for this review — a human should double-check any 'Needs Review' items before deciding.
